steps.title: Use the real-square rule
steps.content: For any real number $x$, $x^2$ is never negative. The smallest possible value of $x^2$ is $0$.

steps.title: Apply it to the equation
steps.content: The equation asks for $$x^2=-841$$ but $-841$ is negative. Therefore, no real value of $x$ can satisfy the equation.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: There are zero distinct real solutions, so choice D is correct. There would be two real solutions for $x^2=841$, but the negative right side changes the result.
